clean_story: Endless returns.  Patterns repeating. "Cycle" comes from the Greek kyklos, meaning "circle"—because what ends loops back to begin again. Think of a bicycle wheel:—each spin lifts you forward, but the motion itself never changes. Nature runs on cycles. Water evaporates, clouds form, rain falls—same molecules, new journeys. Your heartbeat?A cycle of squeeze-and-release, 100,000 times a day. Even time obeys it:Earth’s tilt gives us seasons; its rotation splits day from night. Break the chain, and systems falter—like a clock with no pendulum. Humans harness cycles too. Farmers plant by moon phases. Engines convert fuel’s burn-expand-cool loop into motion. Sleep resets your brain in 90-minute waves. Three related subjects are rhythms in chaos, feedback loops and spiral growth.
